extension.summary: Experiment: no-blip OPPO playback with pure, spec-legitimate HDMI-CEC switching (no IR, no spoofing).
extension.description: CEC Control Experiment is a fork of OppoKodiBridge v3 that keeps v3's playercorefactory handoff (Kodi routes disc content to an external OPPO player, so there is no pre-play "blip") but switches the TV using ONLY legitimate HDMI-CEC. The OPPO grabs its HDMI input via its OWN One-Touch-Play, forced by power-cycling the OPPO (it only asserts active source on a power-ON transition); Kodi reclaims its input by re-asserting its OWN active source. There is deliberately NO IR blaster and NO CEC injection / spoofed initiator (which corrupts the shared bus). The trade-off is the ~20-24s OPPO power-cycle on every handoff, accepted in exchange for zero extra hardware and a clean bus. Installs alongside v2 and v3 (different add-on id) for direct comparison. The OPPO HTTP API is community-reverse-engineered, not an official protocol.
extension.news: 0.4.1 -- Second hardening sweep. A multi-agent adversarial hunt found 9 more real bugs (all in error / edge paths); all fixed + pinned by regression tests (78 total). Highlights: the HTTP stop-watcher now uses a tri-state playback probe so a transient network blip is never mistaken for a stop (no premature mid-playback TV reclaim) and is bounded by read-failure + watch-time ceilings so it can never hang and skip the reclaim; serial control maps every failure (missing termios, termios.error, bad baud, absent POSIX flags) to a non-fatal OppoError; #PON still fires when #POF fails; the guided CEC switch-over test is no longer blocked for serial-control users (and Ping reports the serial cable, not a misleading :23 UNREACHABLE); the desktop CEC Switcher surfaces save failures instead of losing settings silently, and treats a connect-then-reset OPPO as reachable. 0.4.0 -- Bug-fix sweep. An adversarial hunt found 13 real bugs, all in previously-untested branches; all fixed + pinned by regression tests (63 total). Highlights: disc-folder mapping for share-root / trailing-slash / .iso-under-BDMV; HTTP status vocabulary (unknown/no-disc tokens now read idle, so the monitor can't loop forever and miss the reclaim); a dropped :23 socket now raises OppoError instead of a raw OSError (so the reclaim and the HTTP fallback aren't skipped); writer/reader profile-path mismatch (both now masterprofile); playercorefactory.xml backup/remove is marker-guarded (never clobbers a user's own file); XML-escaped paths; serial settings wired into the UI; from_addon honours dataclass defaults for undeclared ids; .cec log tag. 0.3.0 -- Clean modular rebuild: detector / handoff / cec / monitor / orchestrator (single-responsibility). The CEC reclaim now goes straight from the orchestrator to Kodi via JSON-RPC -> script.cecreclaim -> CECActivateSource (no flag, no stale-flag class of bug), still single-shot and never re-asserting. New Settings -> Setup & tests: Ping OPPO, Control test (#QPW), and a guided CEC switch-over test. (0.2.0: explicit single-shot reclaim. 0.1.0: fork of v3, IR removed.)
